steps.title: Identify the general-to-specific relationship
steps.content: The first sentence states that Downs draws on “a variety of Mexican musical genres” — a broad claim. The second sentence immediately names specific examples: corridos, rancheras, and other northern Mexican genres. The transition must signal movement from a general statement to concrete examples.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content: ”Specifically” introduces concrete examples that elaborate on the general claim. The other three—”Therefore,” “In conclusion,” and “Rather”—either suggest logical consequence, closure, or contradiction, none of which apply here. That matches choice A — A is correct.
